title: Cystorchis fusca
taxonomy: Cystorchis fusca is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to Africa. The genus Cystorchis was previously considered a subgenus within the genus Gynacharis. However, it is now recognized as a distinct genus. Cystorchis fusca is classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae.

intro: Cystorchis fusca, also known as the Black Cystorchis, is a species of orchid that is native to tropical regions of Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cystorchis fusca include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions in traditional African medicine.
